sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: After years of travelling, she finally chose a coastal town as her permanent (1) ...... (RESIDE).
The photographer spent weeks capturing the natural (2) ...... (ROUND) of the remote mountain village.
Critics questioned the (3) ...... (SOLID) of the proposal, arguing that it lacked the (4) ...... (ARCHITECT) detail required for planning permission.
Car (5) ...... (MANUFACTURE) have been urged to reduce carbon emissions from their production lines.
Despite its modest appearance from the outside, the apartment proved to be remarkably (6) ...... (SPACE) once you stepped inside.
The library redesign included built-in (7) ...... (SHELF) along every wall to maximise storage.
Engineers had to reinforce the (8) ...... (STRUCTURE) of the old bridge before it could be made (9) ...... (INHABIT) for pedestrians year-round.
Planning regulations restrict the height of new buildings in (10) ...... (RESIDE) neighbourhoods to preserve the character of the area.

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Phrases)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Natalie was **far and away / considerably** the strongest candidate at the audition.
The new café is **nothing / nowhere** near as busy as the one that closed down last year.
This year's harvest was nowhere **near / close** as abundant as the previous one.
You'll have to train **hard / hardly** if you hope to qualify for the regional championships.
The speaker's opening remarks were **quite / a good deal** remarkable and immediately captured the audience's attention.
The more you practise, **so / the** more confident you will become.
After the operation, the patient was finally allowed to roam **free / freely** around the ward.
The surgeon's technique was **absolutely / very** flawless, and the procedure was completed without a single complication.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
There is nothing to support these rumours circulating online and we are seeking legal advice. **foundation**
These rumours circulating online ...... and we are seeking legal advice.

You need to watch the new intern closely to make sure the reports are completed correctly. **sharp**
You need to ...... the new intern to make sure the reports are completed correctly.

It only took us a few hours to drive from Paris to Brussels. **matter**
We drove from Paris to Brussels ...... hours.

Marcus didn't win the scholarship because he was considerably less qualified than Sofia. **deal**
Sofia won the scholarship because she had ...... Marcus.
items.answer: a great deal more experience than

items.sentence:
The charity director was accused of making the campaign message less impactful to avoid controversy. **watering**
The charity director was accused ...... to avoid controversy.

The government's new funding package will allow the renovation of the old station to go ahead. **smooth**
The government's new funding package will ...... the renovation of the old station.

Chen has so much ability that several international academies are keen to recruit her. **so**
Chen ...... athlete that several international academies are keen to recruit her.

The industrial warehouse is strikingly out of place and ruins the appearance of the historic town centre. **blot**
The industrial warehouse is ...... and ruins the appearance of the historic town centre.
items.answer: an ugly blot on the landscape

sections.title: D: Collocations / Expressions
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Come in and make ...... comfortable – the meeting will start in just a few minutes.
If you want a seat in the front row, you'll need to be quick ...... the mark and book early.
She promised to stand by her decision, no ...... what the consequences might be.
The new mayor vowed to be tough ...... antisocial behaviour in the city centre.
The manager announced that the first round of drinks would be ...... the house to celebrate the team's success.
The company's flashy new branding was widely criticised as nothing more than ...... dressing.
The constant roadworks outside the office are driving everyone ...... the bend.
Whether the documentary was accurate is a matter of ...... – some experts disagree strongly with its conclusions.
The hostel was ……………………………………… from the most relaxing place I had ever stayed in during my travels.
The sculptor chose this particular stone for its rough ……………………………………… , which gave the finished piece a unique character.
The final exam was ……………………………………… as demanding as the mock test we had taken the week before.
Before the yoga session began, the instructor asked everyone to spread ……………………………………… across the studio floor.
Emergency services were deployed to assist residents trapped in a heavily ……………………………………… coastal district after the storm.
The family was ……………………………………… from the property after the landlord discovered they had sublet it without permission.
By the time we reached the campsite, it was getting ……………………………………… cold, so we lit a fire straight away.
The mechanic had to ……………………………………… the engine parts thoroughly to remove the layer of grease that had built up over the years.
